articles.title: Vail Resorts Profits Fall as Poor Snowfall Puts Damper on 2025-26 Season
articles.description: Vail Resorts reported its quarterly earnings for Q2 of the 2026 fiscal year ended January 31. According to the press release and 10Q filing from March 9, the company has been struggling with the historically low snowfall affecting many of its key resorts, resulting in lower quarterly earnings and lower visitation numbers. Chief executive Rob Katz described the winter as […] The post Vail Resorts Profits Fall as Poor Snowfall Puts Damper on 2025-26 Season appeared first on SnowBrains .
articles.content: Vail Resorts, which now owns more than 40 ski areas worldwide, revolutionized the business of skiing. | Image: Vail Resorts Vail Resorts reported its quarterly earnings for Q2 of the 2026 fiscal year ended January 31. According to the press release and 10Q filing from March 9, the company has been struggling with the historically low snowfall affecting many of its key resorts, resulting in lower quarterly earnings and lower visitation numbers. Chief executive Rob Katz described the winter as “the most challenging across the Rockies that we have ever experienced,” citing the lowest snowfall in more than 30 years at resorts in Colorado and Utah. Limited terrain and warm temperatures reduced visits and spending across the company’s destination resorts. Robert Katz returned as CEO in May last year in a bid to boost the company’s stagnating business. Related: Robert Katz Returns as Vail Resorts CEO in Surprise Leadership Shake Up The company posted net income of $210 million for Q2 2026, do

articles.description: SAM Magazine—Broomfield, Colo., March 10, 2026— Vail Resorts CEO Rob Katz pointed to “the most difficult weather environment in the Rockies we have ever seen” as the primary culprit for the company’s financial declines in both the second quarter ended Jan. 31, 2026, and season-to-date metrics. Q2 resort reported EBITDA was down 8.3 percent year-over-year and net income was off 15 percent, while season-to-date skier visits as of March 1 were down nearly 12 percent compared to the same period last

articles.title: Vail Resorts Reports Declining Skier Visits — What the Numbers Reveal About the Modern Ski Industry
articles.description: Vail Resorts has reported a significant drop in skier visits during the 2025–26 winter season, highlighting how weather variability and changing travel patterns can affect ski tourism. Yet the company’s pass-based model has helped stabilize revenue despite fewer visitors on the slopes. This article examines what the numbers reveal about the Epic Pass system, resort economics and the broader shifts shaping the global ski industry. The post Vail Resorts Reports Declining Skier Visits — What the Nu
articles.content: Early season operating metrics released by Vail Resorts show a noticeable decline in skier visits across its North American resort portfolio during the 2025–26 winter season. The figures, reported through the company’s investor communications and widely covered by financial and industry media, offer an interesting window into how the modern ski resort business model operates during a difficult winter . While the headline numbers point to a challenging season, the underlying data reveals something more nuanced about how large resort operators manage financial volatility. Skier Visits Down — One of the Sharpest Drops in Recent Years According to season-to-date operating metrics, skier visits across Vail Resorts’ North American mountain resorts were down approximately 20% compared with the previous season . The company attributes the decline primarily to weak early-season snowfall across the Western United States , which significantly delayed terrain openings at several major destinations

articles.title: Vail Resorts Admits Visits & Revenue Down
articles.description: As expected, numbers have fallen after a poor winter for snow in the many of the major US resorts. NEW The post Vail Resorts Admits Visits & Revenue Down appeared first on PlanetSKI .
articles.content: The US resort owner said it is due to a ‘worst-case weather scenario’. The comments come from the CEO, Rob Katz, and follows one of the worst winters for snow in decades in parts of the USA. In a news release for shareholders and investors the company admitted: Skier visits fell 11.9% Total lift revenue down 3.6% Ski school revenue down 8.2% Dining revenue down 8.6% Retail/rental revenue down 5.7% In the second financial quarter the net revenue decreased 4.7% , that’s $53.2m. “This has been the most challenging winter across the Rockies that we have ever experienced with the lowest snowfall levels in more than 30 years for our Colorado and Utah resorts, combined with warmer temperatures, resulting in reduced terrain throughout the quarter and into February,” said Katz in a statement. Vail Resorts’ stock price fell more than 3% after the earnings announcement. Skiing in the USA. Image © PlanetSKI. Vail Resorts said it was pleased with the strength and stability shown by the operating mo

The move folds Berkshire East and Catamount into the ownership structure of Bear Den, an investment vehicle led by the Ken Graham family, while keeping the Schaefer family actively involved. articles.title: Billionaire’s Beef With Vail Resorts Continues
articles.description: Cloudflare's CEO has continued his public criticism of Vail Resorts, with an investor dinner being the latest point of contention.
articles.content: Park City, Utah — There’s this billionaire who really doesn’t like Vail Resorts. You’d think maybe @Vail (dead account). @VailResorts last post was March 2025. Trying hard to find an engaged Vail corporate employee and failing. Which is especially weird because they actively disempower their local employees. Dumb. Dumb. Dumb. — Matthew Prince (@eastdakota) March 17, 2026 Matthew Prince is the co-founder and CEO of Cloudflare and an active member of the Park City community. He’s Utah’s richest man, as Matthew currently has a net worth of around $6.8 billion . Over the past few years, he’s argued that the experience at Park City Mountain Resort sucks. In 2025, he purchased the Town Lift Plaza, which is land surrounding the fixed-grip chairlift. Since then, he’s made his intentions clear: he wants to buy the ski resort. However, Vail Resorts has said they’re not interested in selling one of its most valuable properties. The beef reignited last week after Vail’s second-quarter earnings rep

articles.title: Rob Katz Bought $5 million in Vail Stock on Monday, Still No Plan To Fix Reputation Issues
articles.description: It is no secret that Vail Resorts’ stock price has been struggling for years. After peaking in November 2021, the stock price is now worth roughly a third of what it was after a relentless five-year decline. Vail Daily reported that Vail Resorts CEO Rob Katz recently purchased roughly $5 million in Vail Resorts stock, bringing his holdings to almost […] The post Rob Katz Bought $5 million in Vail Stock on Monday, Still No Plan To Fix Reputation Issues appeared first on SnowBrains .
articles.content: Rob Katz joined Vail Resorts’ stock buyback campaign, recently purchasing $5 million worth of stock. | Image: Vail Resorts It is no secret that Vail Resorts’ stock price has been struggling for years. After peaking in November 2021, the stock price is now worth roughly a third of what it was after a relentless five-year decline. Vail Daily reported that Vail Resorts CEO Rob Katz recently purchased roughly $5 million in Vail Resorts stock, bringing his holdings to almost $40 million. CEOs often buy stock in their company as a show of confidence to investors. This purchase followed a critique by Late Apex Partners in early 2025. This independent investment group pointed out that while Kirsten Lynch was CEO, she did not purchase a single share of Vail stock, and that in the last five years no board member has purchased any stock. According to MarketBeat , almost 95% of Vail’s stock is owned by institutional investors and hedge funds. Related: How The Epic Pass Changed Skiing: A Data Drive

Prince […] The post Park City, UT, Billionaire Matthew Prince Takes Aim at Vail Resorts A
articles.content: Cloudflare CEO Matthew Prince at a conference. | Credit: Forbes Park City, Utah, local billionaire and Cloudflare CEO Matthew Prince is once again expressing interest in purchasing Park City Mountain Resort (PCMR) from Vail Resorts, arguing he could drastically improve the experience for guests and employees. Related: A Battle for Park City Mountain, UT: Billionaire Matthew Prince vs. Vail Resorts Empire On March 9, Vail Resorts reported lower-than-expected Q2 earnings. Prince did not wait long before posting on Twitter (X), “Vail Resorts ($MTN) likely to open tomorrow down to where if you invested 10 years ago you’d have done as well putting your money in a hole. It’s time for a change to become more asset-light, sell off resorts, and allow character and differentiation to return to skiing.” While his prediction did not come true and the share price actually bounced, as investors had potentially anticipated worse news or a lower dividend, while Vail Resorts kept its dividend stable, t

articles.title: Ikon Pass Owner Alterra Mountain Company CEO to Step Down at End of Ski Season
articles.description: Alterra Mountain Company CEO Jared Smith will step down at the end of the ski season, marking a leadership transition at the company behind the Ikon Pass. The change comes as global multi-resort passes and corporate consolidation continue reshaping the ski industry and competition between major resort operators. The post Ikon Pass Owner Alterra Mountain Company CEO to Step Down at End of Ski Season appeared first on The-Ski-Guru .
articles.content: A leadership change is coming to one of the most influential companies in the global ski industry. Jared Smith has announced he will step down as chief executive officer of Alterra Mountain Company at the end of the current ski season, marking a transition at the company behind the Ikon Pass. The Denver-based resort operator said Smith will remain involved during the transition while the company searches for a new chief executive. A Leadership Change at One of the Ski Industry’s Largest Operators Alterra Mountain Company is one of the two dominant corporate players in modern ski tourism, alongside Vail Resorts. Founded in 2018 and headquartered in Denver, Alterra operates and partners with dozens of ski destinations across North America and internationally. The company’s Ikon Pass provides access to more than 70 ski areas worldwide through a mix of owned resorts and partner agreements. Under Smith’s leadership, Alterra continued expanding both its resort portfolio and its global pass n

articles.title: Indy Pass Owner Moves Headquarters to New Hampshire as Black Mountain Becomes Innovation Hub
articles.description: Indy Pass owner Erik Mogensen is relocating headquarters to New Hampshire while turning Black Mountain into an innovation hub designed to help independent ski areas compete in a consolidating ski industry. The post Indy Pass Owner Moves Headquarters to New Hampshire as Black Mountain Becomes Innovation Hub appeared first on The-Ski-Guru .
articles.content: The company behind the rapidly expanding Indy Pass has announced it will relocate its headquarters to Jackson, New Hampshire, while transforming the historic Black Mountain ski area into what it calls an “Independent Mountain Laboratory.” Erik Mogensen, CEO of Entabeni Systems , said the mountain will serve as a real-world testing ground for technologies, operational systems and management strategies designed to help independent ski resorts compete in an industry increasingly dominated by large corporate operators. The announcement includes the buyout of early investors in Black Mountain, returning the resort to 100 percent private ownership under Mogensen’s leadership . A Test Mountain for Independent Ski Areas Black Mountain will operate both as a ski area and as an innovation hub where new software, hardware and operational tools can be developed and tested before being shared with other independent ski resorts. According to Mogensen, many smaller ski areas lack the resources and ti

articles.title: Ski Industry Giants Shake Up Continues: Alterra CEO Jared Smith to Step Down After 2025-26 Season
articles.description: Alterra Mountain Company announced a leadership transition Tuesday, March 10, with Chief Executive Officer Jared Smith planning to step down at the end of the current winter season. Smith said serving as CEO had been “an honor,” noting that the company had expanded significantly during his tenure. “Serving as CEO of Alterra has been an honor, and I am deeply […] The post Ski Industry Giants Shake Up Continues: Alterra CEO Jared Smith to Step Down After 2025-26 Season appeared first on SnowBrains
articles.content: Jared Smith served as CEO at Alterra since June 2022. | Image: Alterra Mountain Company Alterra Mountain Company announced a leadership transition Tuesday, March 10, with Chief Executive Officer Jared Smith planning to step down at the end of the current winter season. Smith said serving as CEO had been “an honor,” noting that the company had expanded significantly during his tenure. “Serving as CEO of Alterra has been an honor, and I am deeply proud of the incredible people, capabilities and businesses we’ve added to this amazing company,” Smith said in the company’s press release. “The commitment of the ownership group to these mountains, and to the team members and communities they serve, is truly unique.” Alterra’s board will now begin a comprehensive search for a new chief executive, while an interim leadership structure will oversee operations through the end of the season. Smith will remain available over the next year to help support the transition. During the transition, an Ex

articles.title: Alterra Mountain Company Announces Departure of CEO
articles.description: During his tenure, Smith continued the growth of Alterra, which included acquisitions like Arapahoe Basin, Schweitzer, and Ski Butlers.
articles.content: Denver, Colorado — In a surprising development, one of the big players in North America’s ski industry is changing up its leadership team. Yesterday, the Alterra Mountain Company announced that Jared Smith will be leaving his position at the end of the 2025-26 ski season. A reason why was not given. “Serving as CEO of Alterra has been an honor, and I am deeply proud of the incredible people, capabilities and businesses we’ve added to this amazing company, ” said Jared Smith. “The commitment of the ownership group to these mountains, and to the team members and communities they serve, is truly unique. I’m confident the progress we’ve made has positioned the company well to build on that foundation in the years of growth ahead.” Jared Smith was first hired as the company’s President in 2021 and then became CEO in August 2022. He’s a bit of an enigma, which is the benefit you have when you’re running a private company. He conducted some interviews during his tenure, including a couple wit

articles.title: Smith to Step Down as Alterra CEO
articles.description: SAM Magazine—Denver, March 11, 2026— Jared Smith plans to step down from his role as chief executive officer of Alterra Mountain Company at the end of this season. An exact end date was not shared. Smith joined Alterra as president in June 2021, reporting to then-CEO Rusty Gregory. He previously served as president and global chairman of Ticketmaster. He succeeded Gregory as CEO on Aug. 1, 2022. During his time at Alterra, the company grew its portfolio of ski areas as well as related businesses

articles.title: Abandoned Ski Resort in Michigan Purchased For $8 Million By Conservancy Group
articles.description: The Leelanau Conservancy completed the acquisition of the former Sugar Loaf Ski Resort following an $8 million fundraising campaign.
articles.content: Cleveland Township, Mich. — An abandoned Northern Michigan ski resort is set to become a public outdoor recreation area after a land conservancy finalized the purchase of the property. MLive reports The Leelanau Conservancy announced March 11 that it completed the acquisition of the former Sugar Loaf Ski Resort following an $8 million fundraising campaign. The 288-acre property will now undergo ecological restoration and be developed for public access. History of Sugar Loaf Ski Resort Plans for the site include a network of hiking and biking trails along with winter recreation such as snowshoeing, cross-country skiing and backcountry skiing. Officials also plan to add universal-access features, a family sledding hill, a nature play area and a summit pavilion overlooking Lake Michigan. Conservation efforts will focus on removing invasive species, reducing erosion and improving local water quality. The Edmund F. and Virginia B. Ball Foundation purchased the property in 2020 through a sub

articles.title: New Amusement Park Giant Has Surprisingly Big Ski Industry Presence
articles.description: A real estate investment trust firm that just acquired various amusement parks has a surprisingly large presence in the ski industry.
articles.content: Kansas City, Missouri — The amusement park industry was forever changed by a major transaction earlier this month. On March 5th, the Six Flags Entertainment Corporation announced the sale of various amusement and water parks to EPR Properties. Six Flags, which merged with Cedar Fair in 2024, has been working on downsizing to invest in its most valuable properties. This has included the closure of one park, the removal of a few popular attractions (RIP Kingda Ka), and now this downsizing. The parks sold include Six Flags Great Escape in New York, La Ronde in Quebec, Michigan’s Adventure (you can probably guess which state that’s in), Schlitterbahn Galveston in Texas, Six Flags St. Louis in Missouri, ValleyFair in Minnesota, and Worlds of Fun in Missouri. In response, a new corporation was created before the sale that will operate these parks (except La Ronde), and a few others run by Innovative Attractions Management. This new company, Enchanted Parks, will be a partnership between EPR

articles.title: Matthew Prince Wants to Buy Park City — Debate Over Vail Resorts’ Ski Model
articles.description: Cloudflare CEO Matthew Prince has renewed his push to buy Park City Mountain Resort, challenging Vail Resorts’ corporate ski model. The post Matthew Prince Wants to Buy Park City — Debate Over Vail Resorts’ Ski Model appeared first on The-Ski-Guru .
articles.content: A renewed attempt by tech billionaire Matthew Prince to acquire Park City Mountain Resort has reignited debate about the future of large corporate ski resort ownership in North America. Prince, the co-founder and CEO of the internet infrastructure company Cloudflare, has again suggested that Vail Resortsshould consider selling its flagship Utah property, Park City Mountain Resort. But beyond the financial details of a potential acquisition, the proposal raises a broader question that has been growing across the ski industry in recent years: can local ownership manage major ski areas better than large corporate resort groups? A Billionaire With Local Ties to Park City Matthew Prince is not an outsider to the Utah ski town. The Cloudflare founder owns property in Park City and has spent significant time in the community. His proposal to purchase the resort has been framed partly as an effort to bring the mountain back under local stewardship rather than corporate ownership. Prince has su

articles.title: Making More of Martigny
articles.description: The valley town has ambitious plans to become an essential stop off on the way to a multitude of Swiss ski resorts. The latest attraction is the 4*Quartier LôDzè. PlanetSKI spends 48-hours in Martigny to experience what is now on offer. NEW The post Making More of Martigny appeared first on PlanetSKI .
articles.content: Most skiers pass through Martigny on the way from Geneva to Zermatt, Verbier, Crans-Montana and other ski resorts in western and central Switzerland. It sits just off the motorway in the Rhone Valley and is on the main railway line. Just a handful of people used to stop, but that is now changing as Martigny has rather reinvented itself. It is a genuine mountain town at an historic crossroads of the Alps between Switzerland, Italy and France. Now it offers much more than people may think. We report on the town itself and the ski resorts nearby, but we start in LôDzè Quartier – the new jewel in the crown of Martigny that represents its changes and its aspirations. LôDzè Quartier Martigny, Switzerland. Image © PlanetSKI LôDzè Quartier is rooted in community and built on design, creativity and wellbeing. A place to eat, rest, connect and restore – Alphonse, Les Bains Publics , Le Cercle restaurant , Borsari hotel and Sauciteque . The project cost CHF 41.5m (£35m) and opened a year ago. LôD

articles.title: One Dead in Swiss Gondola Crash
articles.description: A woman was killed when a gondola came off its cables in Engelberg. An investigation is underway. UPDATED The post One Dead in Swiss Gondola Crash appeared first on PlanetSKI .
articles.content: Police said the gondola detached from the cable “for reasons that are still unclear”. The accident happened on Wednesday morning. Eyewitnesses told Swiss media that strong winds were buffeting the lift when the crash happened. Video on social media shows the gondola tumbling down Mt Titlis and flipping over several times before coming to a halt. The victim was a 61-year-old Swiss woman. The gondola could carry up to 8 people. The cause of the crash is being investigated. The cantonal police said the woman was alone in the Titlis Xpress ski gondola when it detached from the cable. The gondola had just left the Trübsee station and was heading up the middle section of the mountain when the accident happened. Police said air rescue service, ambulances and police officers were involved in the rescue operation. One skier said “extremely strong winds” had left the gondolas swaying, and police spoke of gusts of more than 80km/h (50mph). The gondola service is normally suspended when winds clim
